## Gherkin Feature: Dungeons

8 scenarios covering dungeon visibility and entry.

### Scenarios

1. **View list of available dungeons** - When character has class/race, dungeons tab shows available dungeons filtered by level
2. **Dungeon locked when level too low** - Dungeon below level range minus 2 shows as locked
3. **Dungeon locked when level too high** - Dungeon above level range plus 2 shows as locked
4. **Enter a dungeon** - Clicking available dungeon enters it
5. **View dungeon details with monsters and boss** - Dungeon detail shows monsters, boss, and difficulty
6. **Dungeon shows completion badge** - Completed dungeon shows checkmark
7. **Dungeons tab shows placeholder without class/race** - Without class/race selection, shows message
8. **Available dungeons sorted by difficulty** - Dungeons displayed in order of difficulty
content.title: test: Gherkin scenarios for dungeons feature

items.title: test: Gherkin scenarios for dungeons feature

content.body:
## Gherkin Feature: Dungeon Combat

7 scenarios covering combat simulation.

### Scenarios

1. **Start combat in a dungeon** - Entering a dungeon initiates combat
2. **Combat resolves automatically** - Each monster fight resolves without user input
3. **Win combat against all monsters and boss** - Defeating all enemies shows completion
4. **Lose combat (HP reaches 0)** - HP reaching 0 during combat shows defeat
5. **See partial rewards on defeat** - Defeated player gets 50% XP and 25% gold
6. **View combat log after fight** - Combat log shows each round's damage
7. **Combat log shows player HP after each round** - HP displayed per round
content.title: test: Gherkin scenarios for dungeon combat feature

items.title: test: Gherkin scenarios for dungeon combat feature

content.body:
## Gherkin Feature: Reward System

8 scenarios covering XP, gold, and loot rewards.

### Scenarios

1. **Earn XP from defeating monsters** - Each monster defeat grants XP
2. **Earn gold from defeating monsters** - Each monster defeat grants gold
3. **Receive guaranteed dungeon completion reward** - Completing dungeon grants guaranteed item
4. **Receive boss guaranteed loot** - Defeating boss grants guaranteed rare item
5. **Receive random loot drops** - Monsters have weighted random loot drops
6. **XP triggers level up** - Reaching XP threshold triggers level up
7. **Level up grants stat point** - Level up grants 1 free stat point
8. **Level up increases max HP and max MP** - Level up increases max HP/MP based on class

## Gherkin Feature: Leveling System

7 scenarios covering the leveling mechanics.

### Scenarios

1. **Character starts at level 1** - New character has level 1
2. **Gain XP increases progress** - Gaining XP updates XP progress bar
3. **XP threshold triggers level up** - Reaching XP threshold triggers level up modal
4. **Allocate stat point on level up** - Player can allocate stat point to a stat
5. **Stat point increases chosen stat** - Allocated stat point increases the chosen stat
6. **Max HP increases on level up** - Level up increases max HP based on class hitDie and CON
7. **Max MP increases on level up** - Level up increases max MP based on INT, WIS, and level

content.body:
## Overview

Phase 1 Foundation: Core activity tracking system that allows users to log physical activity sessions. This is the bedrock of the entire gamification loop.

## Scope

- Manual entry for walking, running, cycling, home workout, and free-form activities
- Local persistence of activity sessions
- Rejection of sessions below 5-minute threshold
- View list of past sessions

## Global Specs References

- Section 7: Activity Tracking (types, thresholds, data collected)
- Section 11.2: MVP scope ÔÇö podom├¿tre + saisie manuelle
- Section 14: Phase 1 ÔÇö Foundations (Weeks 1-4)

## Key Constraints

- Session minimum: 5 minutes (Section 7.3)
- No weight/IMC/calorie tracking (Section 7.5)
- No regression, never (Section 1.3)
- Max 2 guaranteed drops per day (Section 5.3)
content.title: epic: activity tracking system

content.body:
## Overview

Drop & reward system: Three-layer reward delivery ensuring every session produces a meaningful drop. Guaranteed rewards, surprise bonuses with pity timer, milestone rewards on floor completion.

## Scope

- Guaranteed reward per session (fragment counter, rotation)
- Surprise bonus with pity timer (baseRate 15%, escalation 15%, max drought 5)
- Milestone reward on floor completion
- Max 2 guaranteed drops per day (anti-farming)

## Global Specs References

- Section 5: Drop Logic (3-layer structure)
- Section 5.2: Bonus surprise formula and parameters
- Section 5.3: Anti-farming rules, no lost rewards
- Section 11.2: MVP ÔÇö complete drop system

## Key Constraints

- Every session must reward ÔÇö never empty-handed
- Max 2 guaranteed drops/day (Section 5.3)
- Pity timer: 100% at 5 sessions without bonus
- No reward conditioned on performance quality
- Lost rewards on crash must be delivered on next open

## Overview

Avatar system: A visual character representation that evolves through equipment and cosmetic unlocks. Inspired by manhwa aesthetics (Solo Leveling, Tower of God).

## Scope

- Avatar customization at creation (morphology, skin/hair palette)
- Visual changes as equipment is equipped
- View avatar screen
- Semi-realistic simplified style, mobile-readable at 80x80px

## Global Specs References

- Section 3: Avatar System & Visual Evolution
- Section 3.3: Initial customization options
- Section 3.4: Evolution exclusively through equipment (no body changes)
- Section 8: Adventure mode / narrative

## Key Constraints

- No body shape changes related to real weight (psychological risk)
- Inclusive morphology options (no implicit body shaming)
- Readable at 80x80px on mobile
- Evocative over detailed (gradients + particles > anatomy)
- 5 base silhouettes x 2 palettes = 10 variations (MVP)

## Overview

Floor progression system: The core RPG loop where accumulated activity sessions drive the player up floors of the Tower. Each floor represents a biome/themed challenge completed through real-world activity accumulation.

## Scope

- Display current floor to the player
- Accumulate session progress toward floor completion
- Floor completion rewards (narrative equipment)
- Logarithmic difficulty curve (Section 2.4)

## Global Specs References

- Section 2: Tower/Floor Progression Architecture
- Section 2.4: Difficulty curve (logarithmic, diversity-based)
- Section 4.4: Narrative equipment on floor completion
- Section 14: Phase 1 ÔÇö Foundations

## Key Constraints

- Unidirectional progression only (no falling back)
- No penalty for inactivity
- Floors completed by accumulation, not performance
- First floors: 2-3 sessions to complete (Section 2.4)

## Overview

Equipment & inventory system: Purely cosmetic and narrative-driven equipment. Items have no numerical stats ÔÇö their impact is visual (avatar appearance) or narrative (unlock paths, dialogues, lore zones).

## Scope

- View inventory screen
- Equip/unequip items
- Rarity understanding (Common, Rare, Epic, Legendary)
- Equipment slots (5 categories: cape/back, weapon/tool, chest, head, feet)

## Global Specs References

- Section 4: Equipment System
- Section 4.2: Modular asset architecture (5 bases x 4 palettes x 3 effects)
- Section 4.3: Rarity hierarchy
- Section 4.4: Narrative equipment (milestone unlocks)

## Key Constraints

- Items are PURELY cosmetic and narrative ÔÇö no ATK/DEF/stats
- No stat system (unbalanceable for solo dev)
- 4 rarity tiers: Common, Rare, Epic, Legendary
- Legendary items never mechanically superior
- 5 base silhouettes for MVP (needs design input)

content.body:
## Overview

NPC & dialogue system: Non-player characters that populate the narrative world, providing emotional connection and re-engagement after absences. NPCs never blame absence ÔÇö they always welcome the player back.

## Scope

- Scripted dialogues for 3 NPCs (5 dialogues each = 15 total)
- Read dialogues without skipping
- Re-engagement NPC after 3+ days absence
- Contextual messages during gameplay

## Global Specs References

- Section 6: NPC & Re-engagement System
- Section 6.2: NPC types (progression + re-engagement)
- Section 6.3: Dialogue generation templates
- Section 8.4: MVP narrative volume (3 NPCs x 5 dialogues)

## Key Constraints

- Maximum 1 re-engagement NPC per absence period
- Only triggers on first reopen after 3+ days
- Tone: friendly, curious, never guilt-inducing
- Dialogue rotation: no template reuse until 5 others displayed
- MVP: 3 NPCs with 5 fixed dialogues each (15 total)

Drop feedback animation: Visual and audio feedback when rewards are delivered. Simple but satisfying animations that make the drop moment feel rewarding and clear.

## Scope

- Animation for guaranteed reward drop
- Animation for surprise bonus drop (distinct visual)
- Animation for milestone/floor completion reward

- Section 14: Phase 2 ÔÇö Weeks 5-8 (visual feedback)
- Section 11.1: Vector-based assets (SVG) for scalability

## Key Constraints

- Simple animations suitable for mobile
- SVG-based for scalability
- Distinct visual for each reward layer (guaranteed vs bonus vs milestone)
- Works at 80x80px avatar scale
- No performance impact on low-end devices

## Overview

Narrative notifications: Passive, curiosity-driven notifications that maintain engagement during player inactivity without guilt-tripping. Always framed as world updates or invitations.

## Scope

- Passive notifications after 2+ days inactivity
- Celebration notifications on floor completion
- Disable notifications option (no penalty)
- Maximum 1 notification per day

## Global Specs References

- Section 9: Notifications & Communication
- Section 9.1: Philosophy - never guilt-trip
- Section 9.2: Notification types (passive, NPC return, celebration)
- Section 9.3: Max frequency (1/day, never before 48h)

## Key Constraints

- NEVER use formulations like you havent come in X days
- Always framed as invitation or world update
- Max 1 notification per day
- No notifications before 48h of inactivity
- Fully disableable without penalty
- Examples: A strange sound echoes from the next floor... (curiosity)

## User Story

As a player, I want a chance to receive a surprise bonus reward after each session, with the probability increasing the longer I go without one, so that rewards feel exciting and never too far apart.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] After guaranteed reward, a bonus check is performed
- [ ] Base bonus chance: 15% per session
- [ ] Chance increases by 15% per session without bonus (15%, 30%, 45%, 60%, 100%)
- [ ] Bonus is guaranteed at session 5 without one (pity timer)
- [ ] Bonus pool includes: rare cosmetic, NPC gift, lore scroll, special color palette
- [ ] Counter resets to 0 after a bonus is received
- [ ] Bonus delivery is visually distinct from guaranteed reward

## Epic Reference

Parent Epic: #78 (epic: drop & reward system)

## Global Specs

- Section 5.2: Bonus surprise formula and parameters
- baseRate: 0.15, escalation: 0.15, maxDrought: 5

content.body:
## Problem

CI BDD tests fail consistently due to **ambiguous step definitions** ÔÇö the same Gherkin step text matches multiple step definition files. Cucumber cannot determine which one to use and aborts.

## Failing Steps (from CI logs)

### 1. 'I am on the character creator page'
Matches 3 different step files:
- `step-definitions/character-creation.steps.ts:4`
- `step-definitions/hp-mp.steps.ts:29`
- `step-definitions/navigation.steps.ts:21`

### 2. 'I am on the "{string}" tab'
Matches 2 different step files:
- `step-definitions/character-creation.steps.ts:9`
- `step-definitions/stat-allocation.steps.ts:4`

## Root Cause

Multiple step definition files define the same step text pattern. This is a test code quality issue ÔÇö each step should be uniquely defined in exactly one file.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] No step definition matches more than one file
- [ ] `npm run test:bdd -- --dry-run` runs without 'Multiple step definitions match' errors
- [ ] CI BDD test step passes (or at least doesn't fail on ambiguous steps)
- [ ] All existing feature files still execute correctly

## How to Fix

For each duplicate step:
1. Decide which file should own the step (the most semantically appropriate one)
2. Remove or rename the step in the other files
3. If the step is needed in multiple contexts, make it more specific (e.g., 'I am on the Character Creator page', 'I am on the HP/MP tab')

## Files to Check

- `my-react-app/tests/step-definitions/character-creation.steps.ts`
- `my-react-app/tests/step-definitions/hp-mp.steps.ts`
- `my-react-app/tests/step-definitions/navigation.steps.ts`
- `my-react-app/tests/step-definitions/stat-allocation.steps.ts`
- Any other step files that may have overlapping step definitions

content.body:
## Problem

BDD tests in CI fail because Playwright browsers (Chromium) are not installed. The `npm install` step installs the npm packages (`playwright`, `@playwright/test`) but does NOT download the browser binaries.

## Root Cause

The CI workflow (.github/workflows/ci.yml) does not include `npx playwright install chromium` before running BDD tests.

When Cucumber's Before hook calls `chromium.launch()`, no browser binary is found, causing the launch to fail silently. The After hook then fails with:
```
TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'close')
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] CI workflow includes `npx playwright install chromium` with `working-directory: my-react-app` BEFORE the BDD test step
- [ ] CI test-and-build job passes (build + tests + BDD)
- [ ] No regression in npm test (Jest unit tests)
- [ ] No regression in npm run build

## Fix Location

File: `.github/workflows/ci.yml`

Add a step between `npm test` and `Run BDD tests`:
```yaml
      - name: Install Playwright browsers
        working-directory: my-react-app
        run: npx playwright install chromium
```

## Notes

This is the final blocker for CI. Issues #126 (ambiguous steps) and #128 (duplicate steps + hooks.ts loading) have been addressed. Once this is fixed, PR #130 should pass CI.

´╗┐## Problem

The Before hook in hooks.ts has two issues:

### 1. Noisy logs
Every scenario prints "Dev server is already running (PID X), skipping server start" ÔÇö 104 times per CI run. The dev-server-runner.mjs already manages server lifecycle, so this check is redundant.

### 2. Broken isProcessAlive function
The function calls `taskkill /F` to check if a process exists, which actually FORCE-KILLS it. On Windows local dev, this kills the dev server before each scenario. On Linux CI, `taskkill` doesn't exist so it silently fails (returning false).

## Fix
Remove the lock file check and isProcessAlive function entirely from hooks.ts. The dev-server-runner.mjs is the sole manager of the dev server lifecycle ÔÇö hooks.ts just needs to set up Playwright browser/page for each scenario.

## Summary

Simplify the engineering-lead agent's workflow from a complex multi-phase pipeline (test ÔåÆ frontend ÔåÆ release) to a clean 2-handoff model (plan ÔåÆ domain agent ÔåÆ review/merge).

## Changes Needed

### 1. Branch Creation Rule
Add mandatory step to always checkout from origin/master:
\\\
git fetch origin master
git checkout -b feature/<issue-number>-<short-desc> origin/master
\\\

### 2. Issue Pipeline ÔÇö 2-Handoff Model (replacing Parallel Work Streams)
Replace the complex 5-phase pipeline (test ÔåÆ implement ÔåÆ verify ÔåÆ docs ÔåÆ ship) with a simpler model:
- HANDOFF 1: You plan ÔåÆ assign domain agent the complete task
- HANDOFF 2: You review ÔåÆ verify build ÔåÆ merge
- Domain agents self-verify (tests + build + docs) in one session

### 3. Receiving Work Section
Simplify to reflect that agents create their own PRs and the engineering lead just reviews/merges.

### 4. Parallel Scheduling Rules
Update to reflect the simpler model (keep 2-3 streams active, never assign same agent twice).

## Why

- Domain agents have all tools (write, edit, bash, read) to self-verify
- Eliminates unnecessary pipeline routing through test ÔåÆ release agents for every issue
- Reduces handoffs per issue from 4-5 to exactly 2
- Aligns with actual workflow (agents already self-verify)

## File to Modify

\.opencode/agents/engineering-lead.md\

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Branch creation rule added: always checkout from origin/master
- [ ] Issue Pipeline section updated from Parallel Work Streams to 2-Handoff Model
- [ ] Receiving Completed Work section simplified
- [ ] PR Review section preserved
- [ ] Master Branch Change Handling section preserved
- [ ] Delegation Table preserved
- [ ] All existing field IDs and commands preserved (must stay accurate)

## Related

- Closes leftover from PR #123 (was too broad, this is the clean take)
content.title: refactor: simplify engineering-lead workflow to 2-handoff model

´╗┐## User Story

As a user, I want to log my real-world exercise sessions and see my progress through the Tower floors so that my fitness effort translates into game progression.

## Stories Covered

This combines stories #84 (walking), #85 (running), #86 (cycling), #87 (free-form), #88 (persistence), #89 (5-min minimum), #90 (home workout), #91 (current floor), #92 (accumulation).

## Acceptance Criteria

### Activity Logging
- Player can log a session from AdventurePage with type (walking, running, cycling, workout, free-form)
- Type selector with icons/labels for each activity type
- Duration input in minutes (minimum 5, validation enforced)
- Optional notes field
- Sessions below 5 minutes rejected with clear error message
- Home workout option available as a type
- Session saved to character state immediately

### Floor Display
- Current floor number shown on AdventurePage top bar
- Progress bar shows sessions completed toward next floor
- Floor label/name displayed alongside floor number

### Floor Progression
- Floor 1 requires 2 sessions to complete
- Floor 2 requires 3 sessions
- Floor 3 requires 3 sessions
- Floor 4 requires 4 sessions
- Floor 5 requires 4 sessions
- Completing a floor advances to the next floor
- Progress persists across page reloads

### Persistence
- Sessions array saved in character state and persisted to localStorage
- Current floor saved and restored

## Technical Details

### State Changes (CharacterContext.jsx)
Add to initial state:
- sessions: [] - array of { type, duration, notes, timestamp, date }
- currentFloor: 1 - current floor number
- currentFloorProgress: 0 - sessions completed toward current floor

Add reducer actions:
- ADD_SESSION - adds session, increments floorProgress
- ADVANCE_FLOOR - increments currentFloor, resets progress
- Modify LOAD_CHARACTER to restore sessions and floor

### Floor Config
Create src/data/floors.js with:
- Floor 1: 2 sessions, name 'Verdant Approach'
- Floor 2: 3 sessions, name 'Ashen Pass'
- Floor 3: 3 sessions, name 'Crystal Depths'
- Floor 4: 4 sessions, name 'Stormspire'
- Floor 5: 4 sessions, name 'The Crucible'
- Helper: getFloorRequirements(floor) returns sessions needed

### UI
Create: src/components/ActivityLogger/ActivityLogger.jsx
Update: src/pages/Adventure/AdventurePage.jsx - add floor display + activity logger

### Data Test IDs
- activity-logger, activity-type-select, activity-duration-input
- activity-notes-input, btn-log-session, current-floor
- floor-progress-bar, activity-error

### Files to Create/Modify
- CREATE: src/data/floors.js
- CREATE: src/components/ActivityLogger/ActivityLogger.jsx + .css
- MODIFY: src/contexts/CharacterContext.jsx
- MODIFY: src/pages/Adventure/AdventurePage.jsx
- MODIFY: src/services/api.js

### Verification
- npm run build must pass
- npm run test:bdd --dry-run must show no errors

´╗┐## User Story

As a player, I want every logged activity session to feel rewarding with guaranteed rewards, occasional surprise bonuses, and meaningful milestone rewards on floor completion.

## Stories Covered

#93 (floor completion reward), #94 (guaranteed session reward), #95 (bonus reward with pity timer), #96 (milestone reward on floor completion).

## Acceptance Criteria

### Guaranteed Session Reward (#94)
- Every logged activity session gives a guaranteed reward
- Reward: gold (scales with session duration: floor(duration * 10) gold)
- Reward is displayed briefly after logging
- Max 2 guaranteed rewards per day (anti-farming, per epic #78 spec)

### Bonus Reward with Pity Timer (#95)
- Base 15% chance for a bonus reward on any session
- Each session without a bonus increases the chance by 15% (pity timer)
- At 5 consecutive sessions without bonus, next session is 100% guaranteed bonus
- Bonus options (random): extra gold, a random common equipment item, a consumable
- Bonus notification is distinct from guaranteed reward

### Floor Completion Milestone (#93, #96)
- When a floor is completed, player gets 500 gold + a random Uncommon/Rare equipment item
- Milestone reward is shown in a brief celebration display
- Floor completion reward is separate from session reward (additive)

### Persistence
- Reward streak/drought counters persist in localStorage
- Today's reward count resets daily
- Bonus pity timer persists across app restarts

## Technical Details

### State Changes (CharacterContext.jsx)
Add to initial state:
- rewardStreak: 0 (consecutive sessions without bonus)
- todayRewardCount: 0 (guaranteed rewards today)
- lastRewardDate: null (date string for daily reset)

Modify ADD_SESSION to:
1. Calculate gold reward = floor(duration * 10)
2. Check anti-farming (max 2/day)
3. Apply pity timer logic
4. Roll for bonus reward
5. Dispatch appropriate rewards

Modify ADVANCE_FLOOR to:
1. Give 500 gold
2. Give a random Uncommon+ equipment item from data/equipment

### Data Test IDs
- reward-display: data-testid='reward-display' on reward notification
- bonus-reward: data-testid='bonus-reward' on bonus notification
- milestone-reward: data-testid='milestone-reward' on floor completion
- pity-counter: data-testid='pity-counter' (if shown)

### Files to Modify
- src/contexts/CharacterContext.jsx ÔÇö reward logic
- src/pages/Adventure/AdventurePage.jsx ÔÇö reward display
- src/components/ActivityLogger/ActivityLogger.jsx ÔÇö success feedback
- src/services/api.js ÔÇö persist new state fields

### Verification
- npm run build must pass
- npm run test:bdd --dry-run must show no errors

## Problem

From Game Design Review (2026-05-16) ÔÇö [Critical] Finding #1

The primary call-to-action "Create Character" button is visually similar in size and color to secondary buttons ("My Characters", "Adventure"), making it hard for new players to identify the main entry point.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] "Create Character" button is visually distinct from secondary buttons (larger, primary color, increased padding)
- [ ] Button has subtle hover/focus animation
- [ ] Visual weight hierarchy: Create Character > My Characters > Adventure
- [ ] Mobile: button remains prominent on smaller screens
- [ ] Color contrast meets WCAG AA on the button text

## Design Direction

- Increase font-size (e.g., 18px vs 14px for secondary)
- Use primary brand color with white text
- Add increased padding (e.g., 16px vertical vs 10px)
- Add subtle box-shadow or glow effect
- Add hover animation (scale 1.05 or brightness shift)

## Files to Modify

- `src/pages/Home/Home.jsx`
- `src/pages/Home/Home.css` (or inline styles)

## Research Basis

Nielsen's first-click recommendation: the most important action should be visually distinct (larger, colored, or spaced) to reduce decision latency.
content.title: fix: make 'Create Character' button visually dominant

## Problem

From Game Design Review (2026-05-16) ÔÇö [Major] Finding #2

The class-card contains numerous data points (name, description, stat bonuses, starting skills, weapon proficiencies, armor proficiencies) presented in a single dense card. This violates Miller's Law (7┬▒2 chunks) and can overwhelm new players.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Class card breaks information into collapsible sections (e.g., "Core Info", "Stats", "Abilities")
- [ ] Default view shows only essential info (name, brief description, stat bonuses)
- [ ] Detailed info (starting skills, proficiencies) revealed via progressive disclosure
- [ ] Collapsed state presents no more than 7 chunks of information
- [ ] Mobile: collapsible sections stack vertically without overflow
- [ ] Expanded sections have clear visual indicators (chevron, +/- icon)

## Design Direction

- Use accordion/collapsible pattern for class details
- Primary card shows: class name, icon, 1-sentence description, stat bonuses
- "Show details" button reveals: starting skills, weapon proficiencies, armor proficiencies
- Consider using tabs within the card as an alternative

## Files to Modify

- `src/components/ClassSelector/ClassSelector.jsx`
- `src/components/ClassSelector/ClassSelector.css`

## Research Basis

Cognitive load research shows that presenting more than 7┬▒2 items at once increases error rates. Miller's Law applies directly to UI information architecture.

## Problem

From Game Design Review (2026-05-16) ÔÇö [Major] Finding #3

Increment/decrement buttons disable correctly, but there is no immediate visual change (e.g., animation or color shift) when a stat value updates, reducing perceived responsiveness.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Stat value displays a subtle scale animation (e.g., 1.0 ÔåÆ 1.1 ÔåÆ 1.0) when value changes
- [ ] Color flash effect on stat value (brief highlight) when updated
- [ ] Animation completes within 300ms (quick enough to feel responsive)
- [ ] Animation is disabled when prefers-reduced-motion is set
- [ ] Both increment and decrement trigger the feedback
- [ ] Mobile: animation visible without being distracting

## Design Direction

- CSS `@keyframes` pulse animation on stat value element
- Brief background color flash (e.g., green tint for increase, blue tint for decrease)
- Use `transform: scale()` for the pulse effect
- Apply via CSS class toggling with JavaScript event listener

## Files to Modify

- `src/components/StatAllocator/StatAllocator.jsx`
- `src/components/StatAllocator/StatAllocator.css`

## Research Basis

Nielsen's heuristic "Feedback": users need clear, timely feedback for each action to feel the system is responsive.
content.title: fix: add visual feedback on stat value change

content.body:
## Problem

From Game Design Review (2026-05-16) ÔÇö [Minor] Finding #4

Color option buttons use CSS variables (`--option-color`) which is good, but the selected state styling (`.selected`) relies on a generic class name that may conflict with other components if naming conventions drift.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Renamed selected state class to `appearance-option--selected` (BEM modifier pattern)
- [ ] All references in JSX updated to use new class name
- [ ] All references in CSS updated to use new class name
- [ ] No visual regression (appearance looks identical after rename)
- [ ] No CSS naming conflicts with other components

## Design Direction

- Adopt BEM naming: `appearance-option` (block) + `appearance-option--selected` (modifier)
- Consider adding `appearance-option--hover` for hover state as well
- Document the naming convention in the component's README or CSS comments

## Files to Modify

- `src/components/AppearanceCustomizer/AppearanceCustomizer.jsx`
- `src/components/AppearanceCustomizer/AppearanceCustomizer.css`

## Research Basis

Consistency heuristic emphasizes stable visual language across the UI. BEM naming reduces collision risk.
content.title: fix: rename selected state class for appearance options

## Problem

From Game Design Review (2026-05-16) ÔÇö [Enhancement] Finding #5

Skill unlocking is tied only to class/race selection; there is no visible "skill points" progression or level-up indicator, which are common RPG conventions for character growth.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] XP bar displayed near skill tree header showing current level progress
- [ ] Level indicator (e.g., "Level 1 / 10") shown alongside XP bar
- [ ] XP bar fills proportionally as player gains XP
- [ ] Visual distinction between filled and empty portions of XP bar
- [ ] Level indicator updates when XP crosses level threshold
- [ ] Mobile: XP bar and level indicator remain readable on small screens

## Design Direction

- Horizontal XP bar with gradient fill (top of skill tree section)
- Level badge next to title (e.g., "Level 5" in a pill badge)
- Consider adding a small XP gain animation when XP increases
- Color scheme: gold/amber for XP bar to match RPG conventions

## Files to Modify

- `src/components/SkillTree/SkillTree.jsx`
- `src/components/SkillTree/SkillTree.css`

## Research Basis

RPG players expect visible progression metrics (level, XP) to feel advancement. This is a genre convention that sets player expectations.

From Game Design Review (2026-05-16) ÔÇö [Minor] Finding #5 (not in prioritized list, but important)

Interactive elements lack explicit `aria-label` attributes; screen-reader users may not understand button functions (e.g., "Create Character").

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] "Create Character" button has `aria-label="Create a new character"`
- [ ] "My Characters" button has `aria-label="View my saved characters"`
- [ ] "Adventure" button has `aria-label="Start an adventure session"`
- [ ] All interactive elements in Home page have descriptive aria-labels
- [ ] CharacterPreview component has aria-labels on interactive elements
- [ ] WCAG 2.1 AA compliance verified for all interactive elements

## Files to Modify

- `src/pages/Home/Home.jsx`
- `src/components/CharacterPreview/CharacterPreview.jsx`

## Research Basis

WCAG 2.1 AA requires all interactive elements to have accessible names for screen reader users.

## Problem

From Game Design Review (2026-05-16) ÔÇö [Enhancement] Finding #7 (not in prioritized list, but important)

On mobile, the equipment slot list becomes vertical, causing long scrolling lists that may be difficult to navigate with thumbs. Touch targets appear narrow.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Mobile equipment slot list uses swipeable carousel or compact card layout
- [ ] Touch targets meet minimum 44x44px (Fitts's Law)
- [ ] Equipment slot icons are larger on mobile with increased padding
- [ ] Swipe gestures work smoothly on touch devices
- [ ] No horizontal overflow on mobile screens
- [ ] Item cards display properly in mobile layout

## Design Direction

- Implement swipeable carousel for equipment slots on mobile
- Increase padding on mobile touch targets (min 44px)
- Consider horizontal scroll with snap points for slot navigation
- Use larger icon sizes on mobile for better thumb reach

## Files to Modify

- `src/components/EquipmentGrid/EquipmentGrid.jsx`
- `src/components/EquipmentGrid/EquipmentGrid.css`

## Research Basis

Fitts's Law recommends larger hit targets (44x44px) for mobile. Thumb reach is limited on phone screens.
content.title: fix: improve mobile equipment grid touch target size
